focus: Adversarial conformance review and corrections to the five iteration-3 body cores
noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new and 1 partially new (0.90), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for reducing the corrections to one testable placement model.
keyQuestions: What exact file/change/test sequence can ship the rewrite without breaking route, intake, proof, handoff, or compatibility contracts?
answeredQuestions: What exact file/change/test sequence can ship the rewrite without breaking route, intake, proof, handoff, or compatibility contracts?
ruledOut: Dropping STATUS=ASK from the literal bodies
Keeping presentation assets as a competing normative prompt authority
Allowing brief-provided values into design-reference token tables
Claiming exact @./ rooting without a live fixture

summary: Keep mode-specific missions and local fields, but move universal lifecycle, intake schemas, output blocks, proof labels, and handoff continuity to the shared contract.
The proposed OK|DEFER|FAIL shorthand regresses the required STATUS=ASK path.
Suffix behavior and prompt ownership must be changed atomically because literal wrapper prompts conflict with current presentation-owned wording.
Audit remains review-only; md-generator alone may write artifacts and only measured values may enter token tables.
Safe shipment requires an include-root live fixture, test-first body invariants, atomic package ownership edits, and suffix/status/mutation negative cases.
Current command contract baseline passed 15 of 15 Node tests with zero failures.
Official docs prove @ file-content inclusion but do not explicitly prove the project-root base of @./.opencode/...; a live fixture remains required.
